spec_match.rationale: The lab now covers the exact Math.sumPrecise iterable-number contract: precise finite summation, decimal-literal rounding, -0 for empty input, NaN/Infinity state, and TypeError for non-number and BigInt elements.

interactive.rationale: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp on Chrome 148: Run all cases, Precision, Edge cases, Invalid inputs, custom input editing, and Evaluate custom input all update the table/readout.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still compares reduce() with Math.sumPrecise, but now does so using spec-correct examples instead of claiming [0.1, 0.2] should equal 0.3.

edge_cases.rationale: The UI now includes empty iterable, conflicting infinities, NaN propagation, string element, and BigInt element cases; custom input '"1", 2' produced native TypeError in browser verification.

references.rationale: The references section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/4790090146643968 plus the TC39 proposal and MDN reference.

design_system.rationale: The changed controls use shared CSS variables, and every local button background override sets a matching text color.

summary: Repaired and verified with chrome-devtools-mcp. Before the fix the page lacked the ChromeStatus link and incorrectly marked Math.sumPrecise([0.1, 0.2]) as failing. After the fix, Chrome 148 rendered 10 total cases with the precise column matching 10/10, custom invalid input returned TypeError, the concept and conformance routes had clean console/network state, and the updated conformance suite reported 8 pass / 0 fail.
